Football News

Premier League 2024/25: How to Find the Best Ticket Deals This Season

ByLiam Hargreaves|18 February 2025  ·  5 min read

Premier League fixtures are among the most sought-after in world football. (Getty Images)

With ticket prices hitting record highs, we break down which platforms offer the best value for the Premier League's biggest fixtures.

Why Premier League Ticket Prices Are at Record Highs

The 2024/25 Premier League season has seen average secondary market prices surge by as much as 22% compared to two seasons ago. Growing global demand — particularly from US and Asian fans visiting the UK — combined with a limited supply of official away allocations has created a perfect storm for resellers. High-profile fixtures such as Manchester City vs Arsenal and the North London Derby regularly command four-figure premiums on the secondary market, making savvy buying more important than ever.

Which Platforms Offer the Best Value Right Now

Our latest price analysis across FootballTicketNet and Awin shows that prices for the same seat can differ by up to 35%. For fans on a tighter budget, setting up price-drop alerts on FootballTicketNet for dates six to eight weeks out remains one of the most reliable strategies.

How to Time Your Purchase for Maximum Savings

Data from the last three seasons reveals a clear pricing pattern: tickets typically peak in the 48-hour window immediately after fixtures are announced, drop by roughly 15% in the following fortnight, and then spike again in the final 72 hours before kick-off as sellers panic-sell. The sweet spot for most fixtures is three to four weeks before match day. Midweek fixtures almost always come in cheaper than weekend equivalents even when the two teams are identical, so flexibility with travel is a real advantage.

Essential Tips to Avoid Scams and Invalid Tickets

The Premier League reports that counterfeit ticket activity has increased 40% since contactless entry was introduced across all grounds. Always verify that a resale platform is a member of the Society of Ticket Agents and Retailers (STAR) before buying. Look for listings that include a visible barcode transfer or mobile ticket delivery to your registered email — postal paper tickets sold days before a match are a significant red flag. If a deal looks dramatically cheaper than every other listing, trust your instincts and walk away.
